summary: This post is simply a table of contents listing for Joel Spolsky's compiled book 'Joel on Software.' It enumerates all the chapters included in the print edition, spanning topics from programming fundamentals and functional specifications to software strategy and management. The list serves as both a promotional announcement and a reference for readers curious about the book's scope. No argumentative content or analysis is present — it is purely a catalog of previously published essays collected into book form.
key_points: The book compiles Joel's most influential blog essays into a single print volume
Topics span a wide range: programming, management, hiring, specifications, strategy, and industry analysis
The collection includes iconic essays like 'The Joel Test,' 'The Law of Leaky Abstractions,' and 'How Microsoft Lost the API War'
The book includes an all-new introduction not previously available on the blog
money_quotes: Contents of the Joel on Software book
key_insight: This post announces the table of contents for the compiled 'Joel on Software' book, showcasing the breadth of topics Spolsky covered across software development, management, and industry strategy.
